qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Suppose there are sorted lists of elements each. The time complexity of producing a sorted list of all these elements is: (Hint:Use a heap data structure) A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Which one of the following in place sorting algorithms needs the minimum number of swaps? A. Quick sort B. Insertion sort C. Selection sort D. Heap sort

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Which of the following sorting algorithms has the lowest worse-case complexity? A. Merge sort B. Bubble sort C. Quick sort D. Selection sort

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: What is the number of swaps required to sort elements using selection sort, in the worst case? A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: The number of elements that can be sorted in time using heap sort is A. B. C. D.

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: Which one of the following is the tightest upper bound that represents the number of swaps required to sort numbers using selection sort? A. ) B. ) C. ) D. )

qids.subject: Algorithms
qids.prose: The minimum number of comparisons required to find the minimum and the maximum of numbers is ________
